values: Quantum Textbook Survey - Public Evidence Snapshot
ARWU 2025 top 100 from ShanghaiRanking public payload
BSc/MSc-level quantum-related course textbook evidence found in public web sources.
This is not a complete global census. It is a traceable public-evidence register; LMS, bookstore, and departmental private data remain unavailable without access or outreach.
Batch search access was rate-limited. The workbook therefore separates verified rows from candidates and records gaps for unverified universities.
Official current/course syllabus or course page explicitly matched a textbook or reading.
Official academic source matched a course/readings page but with weaker textbook wording.
Academic domain or official-looking page with textbook evidence from snippet/source metadata.
Third-party or archived/candidate source with textbook evidence.
Low-confidence or non-course source; do not use in headline counts.
For universities in Gaps, check official reading-list systems, bookstores by course code, department course pages, and then email departments/instructors.

values: This document provides information about the Physics 143b: Quantum Mechanics II course offered at Harvard University in Fall 2010, including an outline of topics to be covered over the term. The course is intended as the second part of an introductory quantum mechanics course, assuming familiarity with concepts from Part I of the textbook . Over the term, the course will cover advanced topics ...
PDF Physics 253a: Quantum Field Theory I Harvard University, Fall 2022 ...

Course Description: Path integral formulation of quantum mechanics , Lagrangian per-turbation theory, regularization, renormalization, and counter terms. Relativistic par-ticles and eld operators. 4 theory. Green functions. Feynman rules. The S-matrix. LSZ reduction formula. Particles with spin. Fermions. Gauge elds. Quantum elec-trodynamics and its applications.
PHYSICS 143B - Quantum Mechanics II at Harvard University - Coursicle

PHYSICS 143B at Harvard University (Harvard) in Cambridge, Massachusetts. Introduction to path integrals, identical particles, many-electron theory, WKB approximation, time-dependent perturbation theory, scattering theory, relativistic quantum mechanics , and basics of quantum information.
ENGSCI 170 - Engineering Quantum Mechanics at Harvard University ...

ENGSCI 170 at Harvard University (Harvard) in Cambridge, Massachusetts. As a first course in quantum mechanics , tailored for engineering, applied mathematics and computer science students, this course will teach concepts needed to engineer a quantum world, to understand quantum mechanical properties of materials and build an intuition for quantum information science. The course will be a ...
PHYS 251a : Quantum Mechanics - Harvard University - Course Hero
Access study documents, get answers to your study questions, and connect with real tutors for PHYS 251a : Quantum Mechanics at Harvard University .

Welcome to efollett.com, serving K-12 and US & Canada Colleges and Universities. Here you can rent, buy and download textbooks; get cash for books online and shop for college apparel, gifts, dorm supplies and more.
Intro to Quantum Mechanics: Stanford University

Welcome to efollett.com, serving K-12 and US & Canada Colleges and Universities. Here you can rent, buy and download textbooks; get cash for books online and shop for college apparel, gifts, dorm supplies and more.
No textbook is required , but the instructor's book 'Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers' is available as an optional resource.
Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers | Course | Stanford Online

The text Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers (Cambridge, 2008) is recommended for the course, though it is not required. It follows essentially the same syllabus, has additional problems and exercises, allows you to go into greater depth on some ideas, and also contains many additional topics for further study.
Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers | Cambridge Aspire website

David A. B. Miller, Stanford University, California ... If you need a book that relates the core principles of quantum mechanics to modern applications in engineering, physics, and nanotechnology, this is it. Students will appreciate the book's applied emphasis, which illustrates theoretical concepts with examples of nanostructured materials, optics, and semiconductor devices.
Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers 2 | Course | Stanford Online

This course covers key topics in the use of quantum mechanics in many modern applications in science and technology, introduces core advanced concepts such as spin, identical particles, the quantum mechanics of light, the basics of quantum information, and the interpretation of quantum mechanics, and covers the major ways in which quantum mechanics is written and used in modern practice.
Applied Quantum Mechanics I | Course | Stanford Online

The continuing evolution of quantum ... and subatomic level. This course introduces concepts and techniques that tackle the inherent challenges of modern devices and systems that exploit quantum effects ....
This course introduces the basics of quantum computing.
Free Course: Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers 2 from Stanford University | Class Central

The course is designed to build on a first course on quantum mechanics at the junior or senior college level, so students should have at least that background. The material here is specifically matched to follow on from the Stanford Online QMSE-01 "Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers" class, and all the material from that class is provided as background in the online course materials here.
Free Course: Quantum Mechanics for Scientists and Engineers 1 from Stanford University | Class Central
This course is a substantial introduction to quantum mechanics and how to use it.
Syllabus | Quantum Mechanics I | Chemistry | MIT OpenCourseWare
